title: What to know about fever before using medicinal herbs
intro: Ideal herbal teas for fever include chamomile, ginger, and yarrow. Chamomile is a soothing option, promoting relaxation and aiding sleep. Its anti-inflammatory properties help reduce feverish symptoms. Ginger, renowned for its warming effects, stimulates circulation and perspiration, assisting the body in eliminating toxins. Yarrow acts as a diaphoretic, encouraging sweat production and cooling the body. These three teas, with their natural healing properties, bring comfort and relief during a fever.
definition: A fever is an elevation in the body's normal temperature, typically above 38°C (100.4°F), indicating an ongoing immune response to an infection or illness. Its significance extends beyond mere discomfort, as it plays a vital role in combating harmful pathogens. The heightened temperature creates an unfavorable environment for bacteria and viruses, thereby accelerating recovery and promoting overall well-being. Hence, a fever should not be suppressed indiscriminately, but rather viewed as a crucial ally in the body's defense mechanisms.
